stimulus: Detailed maps of the ocean floor, ______blank unusually steep gradients along the Pacific subduction zones.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

answerOptions.content: the marine geologists studying tectonic boundaries observed bathymetric charts with
tectonic boundary observations show that bathymetric charts of marine geologists have
bathymetric charts reveal that, when studying tectonic boundaries, marine geologists observe
marine geologists studying tectonic boundaries observe bathymetric charts containing

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The convention being tested is subject-modifier placement. This choice makes the noun “bathymetric charts” the subject of the sentence and places it immediately after the modifying phrase “Detailed maps of the ocean floor.” In doing so, this choice clearly establishes that bathymetric charts—and not other nouns or noun phrases in the sentence—are detailed maps of the ocean floor.

Choice A is incorrect because it results in a dangling modifier. The placement of “the marine geologists studying tectonic boundaries” immediately after the modifying phrase illogically suggests that the geologists are detailed maps of the ocean floor. Choice B is incorrect because it results in a dangling modifier. The placement of “tectonic boundary observations” immediately after the modifying phrase illogically suggests that the observations are detailed maps of the ocean floor. Choice D is incorrect because it results in a dangling modifier. The placement of “marine geologists studying tectonic boundaries” immediately after the modifying phrase illogically suggests that the geologists are detailed maps of the ocean floor.
